data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children once more; third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260515T114017-d6756cdd/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing child IDs, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a success. Do not mix implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260515T114017-d6756cdd/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

`.ddx/executions/20260515T114017-d6756cdd/no_changes_rationale.txt` must contain one:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` reject.

To request orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260515T114017-d6756cdd/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260515T114017-d6756cdd/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and project defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
  <bead id="fizeau-5a68ab63">
    <title>repo-structure: extract sticky route leases and route-status scheduler state</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
Sticky route leases, route-status override filtering, and refresh scheduling remain coupled to root service implementation files instead of living behind internal route-health/status mechanics.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
`docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-008-service-package-and-transcript-boundaries.md:43-64` places concrete routing/status mechanics under internal packages. `service.go:18-39` keeps `ResolveRoute` and `RouteStatus` as the public facade boundary. Lease and scheduler implementation remains in `service_route_leases.go:1`, `service_refresh_scheduler.go:1`, and `service_routestatus.go:1`.

PROPOSED FIX
Move sticky lease state, lease matching/expiry, route-status override filtering, and refresh scheduler state into an internal implementation package, preferably `internal/routehealth` unless existing local structure indicates a narrower internal status helper. Keep root methods as adapters preserving public route-status vocabulary.

NON-SCOPE
Do not extract provider/model listing surfaces (`ListModels`, `ListProviders`, `HealthCheck`) except for adapter touch needed to keep compilation green. Do not refresh website docs or changelog in this slice beyond incidental references needed by tests.

GOVERNING ARTIFACT / DEPENDENCY
Governing artifact: ADR-008 (`spec:ADR-008`). Parent bead/dependency: `fizeau-cd9af16b`.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. `service_route_leases.go`, `service_refresh_scheduler.go`, and route-status lease/override code no longer own sticky lease or scheduler mechanics; root code delegates to internal implementation while public CONTRACT-003 route/status types remain at root.
2. Public behavior and JSON shape for sticky route decisions, lease reuse/expiry, route-status override timestamps, and route-status filtering remain unchanged.
3. Focused sticky lease tests pass: `go test . -run 'TestResolveRouteStickyLease.*' -count=1`.
4. Focused CLI status test passes: `go test ./agentcli -run 'TestRouteStatusOverridesSinceFilter' -count=1`.
5. Internal route-health tests pass: `go test ./internal/routehealth/... -count=1`.
6. Full repository gate passes: `go test ./... -count=1`.
7. Hook gate passes: `lefthook run pre-commit`.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>phase:implementation, area:repo-structure, kind:refactor, spec:ADR-008, area:routing, area:status</labels>
    <metadata parent="fizeau-930b498b" base-rev="2affe5629c7752f5171023a6a4bdc5dd5edacd50" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260515T114017-d6756cdd"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. The bead description above is the primary contract. If it names files, specs, or prior beads, read those before editing. Ground decisions in what is already in the repository; do not guess.</note>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
Extracted the sticky-route state, route-decision cache, and refresh scheduler into `internal/routehealth`, with the root service reduced to adapters in [service_route_leases.go](/var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-fizeau-5a68ab63-20260515T114017-d6756cdd/service_route_leases.go), [service_refresh_scheduler.go](/var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-fizeau-5a68ab63-20260515T114017-d6756cdd/service_refresh_scheduler.go), and [service_routestatus.go](/var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-fizeau-5a68ab63-20260515T114017-d6756cdd/service_routestatus.go). Added direct internal coverage in [internal/routehealth/sticky_state_test.go](/var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-fizeau-5a68ab63-20260515T114017-d6756cdd/internal/routehealth/sticky_state_test.go) and updated the existing service tests to use the delegated state.

Verified with `go test . -run 'TestResolveRouteStickyLease.*' -count=1`, `go test ./agentcli -run 'TestRouteStatusOverridesSinceFilter' -count=1`, `go test ./internal/routehealth/... -count=1`, `go test ./... -count=1`, and `lefthook run pre-commit`. Commit: `93c2b49b` with subject `refactor: move route status mechanics into routehealth [fizeau-5a68ab63]`.
